Okay, so I wanted to provide this because I was making this portal and I was like, wow, there's all these meetings that they're going to have. And then I was like, wow, none of these meetings matter if they don't show up the right way. Okay, in fact, who you are, what you worry about, what goes on your head, it's going to be amplified by having more communication with your teams. It's going to become more of a problem if it's a problem right now. And if it's a strength, it'll become more of a strength. Here's the fundamentals that you need to remember. One, it is not what you say, but how you say something matters more than what you say. So how you are talking to your team matters much more than what you are saying. It is the difference between angry and disappointed. The difference between happy and motivating. The difference between disappointed and neutral. So what that comes down to is tone. And so I do suggest that you record your meetings because you will be able to watch yourself. And when you are not in the same state that you are conducting the meetings and you'll see how you solve problems, how you handle people's responses, and you'll be able to coach yourself on it. Okay. And so it is so important that you remain neutral, that you are not angry, that you are not emotional. You have chosen to be a business owner, which means you don't get to choose emotion anymore. You must be logical. And so when you're on these meetings, you need to remember. So something I used to do in the beginning when things were changing and we were growing, and I'd never managed people before. And we went from like one person to 20 within three months. And I was so overwhelmed. There were mornings I woke up and I was just crying because I was stressed out. Because I was like, I need to hire more people. Oh my gosh, but I have to run this business, right? I woke up and every day I had a little sticky note on my computer and it just said, be neutral. So every time I showed up to a meeting, I was like, no matter what happens on this meeting, no matter what news they present me with, no matter what person did what, I will be neutral. And I had this little sticky note right next to my camera where I stared at them through the screen. So every second I would catch myself. So I suggest you do something like that for yourself if you feel that you are the traditional emotional entrepreneur. Two, be prepared. Prepare for meetings or expect for your time to be wasted. So if you don't prepare ahead of time, and one, you're setting an example for your team by coming prepared, meaning you have a list of your priorities for the week and notes on their weekly reports. So every week, if you're going through their weekly reports, making notes to discuss on the meeting, right, as well as bringing up things that you see coming up, like you want to show up prepared for that meeting, you don't want to be blindsided by anything. Okay. Otherwise it's going to be a huge waste of your time. It's going to take way longer than you need to take. All right. Three, do not be a micro manager. Okay. So when people are going through meetings, like you want to delegate, you want to assign deadlines and ownership. You do not want to go through and micro manage every single thing they're doing. All right. So don't pick somebody apart on a meeting. However, you don't want to be a pushover. Okay. So somebody hasn't gotten something done three weeks in a row. You need to identify the fact that it's not working. And so clearly you need to change something. Right. And then lastly, I want you to know that you will never feel like you're perfect at this. You will always feel every time you get off a meeting, there will be, I probably have one meeting a month that I come off and I just feel like a freaking crushed it. Right. I'm like, I was so great on that meeting. Usually there's stuff going on. And so there's, I can pretend like I'm doing awesome on a meeting, but I have in the back of my head this doubt about something and you know, I'm like, is that seeping through the meeting, whatever, or somebody might have done something so off that caught me so far that I even with my little sticky note, I'm like showing anger on a meeting. So I want you guys to remember like, you're never going to be perfect. You just got to do it and you've got to be willing to suck at it. Okay. Because I freaking sucked at meetings. I used to suck at showing up and it just takes practice. And to do that, you have to be vulnerable and put yourself out there.
